description: 9:16 list-explainer with sage 3D chrome cards, two-voice serif typography, numbered cascade opener, whip-blur transitions, comment-keyword CTA. Greg-Isenberg-style format adapted to a modern-creator brand (sage palette + naturally-graded speaker by default — see BRAND.md.example).
fits_script_when: script enumerates a list of 3+ items (tools / concepts / steps / reasons / layers)
speaker is on camera for narration, not voiceover-over-screen-recording
topic is tech / SaaS / AI / productivity / vibe-coding tooling
ends with engagement CTA in the form 'comment X' or similar two-word keyword
does_not_fit_when: no enumeration in the script (single-narrative essay or hot-take)
live screen recording is essential (use screen-face-split)
magazine-style chapter framing or print-layout aesthetic (use editorial-magazine)
ad-style headline + product surface required (use screen-face-split)
speaker is walking-and-talking outdoors (no archetype yet covers this)

patterns.name: Cube hovering over speaker's mouth
patterns.description: Opening 1.5s hook beat. A 3D sage cube (or cube labeled with the topic word, e.g. 'AI') hovers in front of the speaker's mouth, slightly rotating. Sets the topic via object metaphor.

trigger.script_signal: video opens with a single declarative noun-phrase that names the topic (e.g. 'the AI agent stack', 'Claude Code', 'context engineering')

label_text.description: Word printed on the cube face. Usually 1-3 chars.

rotation_amplitude_deg.description: How far the cube rotates on its sine loop

examples.note: The original. 'AI' cube on speaker's mouth.

patterns.name: Numbered cards cascading diagonally
patterns.description: 5 sage 3D cards (numbers 1-5) drop in diagonally from upper-left to lower-right with stagger. Establishes 'this is a list' upfront. Maps to script's enumeration count (default 5, scales 3-7).

trigger.script_signal: speaker introduces a list of N items in first 0-10s (e.g. 'here are the 5 things', 'I use 3 tools', 'the full stack is')

card_count.description: Number of cards in the cascade (3-7). Auto-detect from script enumeration.

stagger_ms.description: Delay between each card's entry, in ms

examples.note: Pure B-roll variant; no speaker, full-screen card cascade

patterns.name: Two-voice serif section header (structural + italic emphasis)
patterns.description: Section title appears: a structural noun-phrase in dark-green serif + an italic-sage emphasis word. Lands with stagger between the two voices. Marks the start of a new layer/section.

trigger.script_signal: speaker transitions to a new section, often with phrasing like 'first, the X', 'next is the X', 'and then there's X'

structural_text.description: Voice A — dark-green serif words. Usually 1-4 words.

emphasis_text.description: Voice B — italic-sage emphasis word/phrase. Usually 1-3 words.

drop_shadow.description: Subtle warm-orange shadow under the type

patterns.name: Tool reveal card with logo
patterns.description: Speaker stays on screen; a 3D sage card with a real product logo (extruded white linework or actual logo PNG) and the tool's name in white serif drops in beside the speaker. Used to introduce one of the tools in the list.

trigger.script_signal: speaker names a specific product (Cursor, Claude Code, Perplexity, Notion, etc.) and credits it with a job

tool_name.description: Product name printed under the logo (e.g. 'Cursor')

logo_path.description: Path to product logo PNG/SVG. Flag as asset-needed if not provided.

card_position.description: Where the card floats relative to speaker: top-right | top-left | center

examples.note: Cursor diamond logo top-right while speaker explains

patterns.name: Liquid-glass card overlay on a backdrop
patterns.description: Frosted-glass card with corner-bracket frame slides in over a backdrop (could be a fake-UI screenshot, a flowchart, or a sage gradient). Used to demonstrate WHAT a tool does in one sentence. Caption sweeps across the glass card.

trigger.script_signal: speaker explains in one sentence what the tool DOES — the verb+object phrase

backdrop_type.description: What sits behind the glass: sage-gradient | screenshot | flowchart

backdrop_asset.description: Image/SVG for backdrop if type=screenshot or flowchart

caption_text.description: The concept-explaining sentence — italic-serif sweep across the glass

examples.note: Glass card over Perplexity flowchart with 'that go and find information' caption

patterns.name: Fake-UI window with code/JSON/list
patterns.description: Light-mode card styled as an app window (chrome dots, title bar, content area). Shows JSON code, list rows, or chat UI. Used to demo a tool's actual surface without needing a real screen recording.

trigger.script_signal: speaker references a specific output format (JSON, markdown, list, chat) or a tool's UI explicitly

content.description: The text content to render inside the fake UI

examples.note: JSON window with url/markdown/json/screenshot keys + 'Scraping...' status pill
Claude Code agent harness sidebar list

patterns.name: Vertical whip-blur transition with chromatic fringe
patterns.description: Hides a scene cut between two B-roll beats. Vertical (or diagonal) directional blur with a chromatic-aberration streak overlay. ~0.5-0.7s total (out + in).

trigger.script_signal: scene cut between two non-speaker beats — used between sections of the list

examples.note: Whip from Claude Code sidebar to search bar reveal

patterns.name: Stack of product-mode cards recomposing
patterns.description: Multiple iOS-style cards stack on top of each other; top card slides up + fades while a new card grows from beneath. Used when ONE tool has multiple modes (e.g. Firecrawl Scrape → Crawl → Extract).

trigger.script_signal: speaker enumerates 2-3 modes/capabilities of a single tool

card_count.description: Number of cards in the stack (2-3)

patterns.name: Outro stacked-words bridge
patterns.description: Multiple words appear at staggered y-positions and varying type sizes ('how' / 'to turn this' / 'into'). Bridges from the layer-walkthrough into the CTA.

trigger.script_signal: speaker says a transitional phrase that frames the CTA — typically 'how to turn this into' or 'if you want to'

phrase.description: The full phrase, will be word-split and staggered

patterns.name: Comment-keyword CTA card
patterns.description: Final 1.5-2.5s. Two-voice serif text — structural verb (Voice A dark-green) + a quoted keyword (Voice B sage italic). Speaker may smile underneath. Hold to the end of the reel.

trigger.script_signal: speaker explicitly asks viewer to comment a keyword to receive something (a doc, a course, a DM)

keyword_text.description: Voice B — the quoted keyword (e.g. 'AI Stack', 'Vault')

examples.note: 'Comment' + 'Stack' on cream paper, no speaker shown

patterns.name: Italic-serif karaoke captions (default fallback)
patterns.description: When no other pattern matches the beat, this is the safety net. Italic-serif white captions with single-emphasis-word color highlight (--primary-mid by default). Speaker stays on camera.

trigger.script_signal: fallback when no other pattern scores above 0.5 — used for connector beats, simple claims, transitions without dedicated patterns

emphasis_words.description: Comma-separated list of words to highlight in --primary-mid (rest stay dim white)

examples.note: Used as the cohesive caption layer running underneath all other patterns

patterns.name: Fake terminal with speaker PIP
patterns.description: Full-frame fake terminal showing the agent working — typed prompt at top, streaming green-checkmark log lines below, occasional progress bar. Speaker shrinks to a chrome-corner picture-in-picture (~25% width, top-right or bottom-right). Used to demonstrate AI doing the actual work the speaker is describing.

trigger.script_signal: speaker describes a tool/agent doing work autonomously — claims like 'AI cut the filler words', 'Claude wrote this code', 'the agent ran for X minutes'

prompt_text.description: The fake user prompt typed at the top of the terminal (e.g. 'remove filler words and dead air')

log_lines.description: Newline-separated log lines streaming below the prompt (each prefixed with checkmark or arrow). Sage-light text on forest-near-black bg. Warm-orange flash on each line entry.

pip_corner.description: Where the speaker PIP sits: top-right | top-left | bottom-right | bottom-left

waveform_strip.description: Optional: render a tiny audio-waveform strip across the bottom that 'redacts out' chunks in real-time as cuts land

examples.note: Real terminal IDE reference — under modern brand, swap to --primary-mid and use Voice C mono (Geist Mono / Roboto Mono).

patterns.name: Hand-drawn cream-paper flowchart
patterns.description: Cream paper backdrop with hand-drawn-feel arrows and pill-buttons. Steps draw on left-to-right (or top-to-bottom) as the speaker says each one. Italic-serif Voice B labels above each pill. The 'punchline' pill (the answer / the winning step) is colored --accent-warm while the rest are sage tones. Replaces sage-stack's numbered cascade for scripts that explain a workflow rather than enumerate items.

trigger.script_signal: speaker walks through a multi-step process or workflow — phrases like 'first I do X, then Y, then Z', 'the flow is', 'I just record, give it to the AI, and it does the rest'

steps.description: Comma-separated step labels in order (e.g. 'Phone Record,AI,Final Cut'). Each becomes a pill-button.

punchline_index.description: Which step (0-indexed) is the 'punchline' — that one gets --accent-warm instead of sage

pill_labels_above.description: Optional comma-separated italic-serif labels above each pill (Voice B emphasis), aligned 1:1 with steps. E.g. 'I record,one command,~5 minutes'

examples.note: Reference whiteboard canvas with mascot + buttons. Under modern brand, drop the mascot, replace cyan/oxblood/navy buttons with sage palette + warm-orange punchline.

patterns.name: Monospace prompt-chip on cream backdrop
patterns.description: Center-frame dark pill containing a monospace command typing in with cursor blink. Below it, a small italic-serif Voice B label names the meta-claim ('one command', 'natural language', 'just type'). When the typing finishes, a small green check fades in next to the pill and the cursor stops. Used to land the 'this is how easy it is' beat.

trigger.script_signal: speaker punches a 'one command' / 'just type X' / 'all I do is run X' claim — emphasizing the simplicity of invocation

command_text.description: The monospace command rendered in the pill (e.g. 'claude edit my-video.mp4')

eyebrow_label.description: Voice B italic-sage label below the pill

type_speed_cps.description: Characters per second for the typing animation

show_check_at_end.description: Whether to fade in a sage-mid checkmark next to the pill when typing completes

background_timeline_strip.description: Optional: faint timeline strip behind the pill showing raw clips collapsing into one continuous bar — the 'merged into final' visual

examples.note: Reference: dark pill with 'make me an intro video' in monospace, cyan eyebrow 'NATURAL LANGUAGE'. Under modern brand, swap cyan eyebrow to --primary-mid italic-serif.

open_questions: Should hook-cube-on-face be optional? Some sage-stack reels open without it (DWcIFs7EVIh skips the cube and goes straight to the cascade). May need a 'hook-cascade-direct' alt.
fake-ui-screen-mock is currently one big pattern — should it split into json-window / chat-prompt / sidebar-list as separate patterns? Each has different layout needs. Defer to v0.2.
Folder-tab section marker (used in the 'web data layer' beat of DXhnRKxERkj) is not yet a pattern. It only appeared once. Watch for second example before committing it as a pattern.
